systemOverview.description: The Client-Provided Data Enhancement extends Stock Buddy's MCP server to accept market data directly from MCP clients, eliminating the need for server-side data fetching. This creates a flexible, secure architecture where clients control their data sources while leveraging Stock Buddy's analysis capabilities.
keyComponents: Client Data Provider - Processes data from MCP tool arguments
Enhanced Tool Schemas - Detailed data requirements for each tool
Validation Layer - Ensures data quality and security
Provider Factory - Selects appropriate provider based on input
Backward Compatibility Layer - Maintains existing provider support

architecturalPrinciples: Data source agnosticism - Support any client data source
Zero server-side storage - No data persistence between calls
Fail-fast validation - Catch data issues early
Backward compatibility - Don't break existing integrations
Security by default - Validate and sanitize all inputs

componentDesign.name: ClientDataProvider
componentDesign.description: Implementation of DataProvider interface that extracts data from MCP tool arguments
responsibilities: Transform to standard data contract format

componentDesign.name: EnhancedToolRegistry
componentDesign.description: Extended registry with detailed data schemas

componentDesign.name: ProviderFactory
componentDesign.description: Selects appropriate data provider based on input

dataFlow: Client prepares data according to tool schema
Client calls MCP tool with data in arguments

dataFormats.name: OHLCV
dataFormats.format: Array of {date, open, high, low, close, volume}

dataArchitecture.storageStrategy: No server-side storage - all data is transient per request

apiDesign.errorHandling: Structured error responses with remediation hints

securityArchitecture.authorizationModel: All authenticated clients have full tool access

architecturalDecisions.title: Use passthrough pattern for client data
architecturalDecisions.context: Need to support client-provided data without server-side storage
architecturalDecisions.decision: Implement ClientDataProvider that passes data directly from arguments to skills
architecturalDecisions.rationale: Simplest approach that maintains statelessness and security

architecturalDecisions.title: Validate data at entry point
architecturalDecisions.context: Client data could be malformed or malicious

architecturalDecisions.title: Maintain backward compatibility
architecturalDecisions.context: Existing users rely on file and database providers
architecturalDecisions.decision: Use provider factory pattern to support both old and new approaches

architecturalDecisions.title: Use JSON Schema for validation
architecturalDecisions.context: Need standardized way to define and validate data contracts
architecturalDecisions.decision: Define tool input requirements using JSON Schema
architecturalDecisions.rationale: Industry standard with good tooling support
